text.title: Accessibility Evaluation Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying accessibility-evaluation; each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Agent inserts alt="Description" placeholders that satisfy automated checks but fail SC 1.1.1 for context-specific images.
antipattern.root-cause: Placeholder passes attribute-present check; the alt-equivalent-information criterion is harder to test.
antipattern.fix: Require the agent to cite the source content the alt text is derived from; reject placeholders.

antipattern.symptom: Contrast fix in light mode silently creates a new violation in dark mode.
antipattern.root-cause: Token-layer edits propagate across modes; CI runs only light mode.
antipattern.fix: Regenerate both modes; CI compares contrast in both before approval.

antipattern.symptom: Agent mass-adds aria-label to buttons that already have visible text labels with different wording — creates SC 2.5.3 violation.
antipattern.root-cause: Auto-fix script does not check accessible-name vs visible-text consistency.
antipattern.fix: Lint accessible-name vs visible-text mismatch before applying any aria-label.

antipattern.symptom: Stakeholder claims AA conformance based on a Stage-1 axe-clean report.
antipattern.root-cause: Tools miss 60-70% of issues; stages 3+4 skipped.
antipattern.fix: Block conformance claim unless Stages 1-5 all signed off with evidence.
